channel.title: robot.tv News
channel.description: Video-first robot news and robotics news from robot.tv covering humanoids, physical AI, quadrupeds, and deployment signals.

item.title: Travis Kalanick launches a new company called Atoms focused on robotics
item.description: Travis Kalanick, the former Uber CEO, has launched a new robotics company named Atoms. The venture's specific focus and initial product details remain undisclosed.

item.title: Don’t miss Neuralink pioneer Noland Arbaugh keynote at the 2026 Robotics Summit
item.description: Noland Arbaugh, a pioneering Neuralink patient, is confirmed as a keynote speaker for the 2026 Robotics Summit. His firsthand experience with a brain-computer interface offers a unique perspective for the robotics community.

item.title: Robot Dogs Are Protecting Data Centers. Operators Are Seeing Payoffs.
item.description: Legged robots are now patrolling data center floors, moving beyond pilot programs into active security and inspection roles. Early operators report measurable returns on investment from these deployments.

item.title: Humanoid robotics developers must address a wide range of applications
item.description: Argues humanoid robotics developers must look beyond a single killer app. Success hinges on designing versatile platforms capable of addressing a wide range of industrial and commercial applications.

item.title: Rivian’s RJ Scaringe thinks we’re doing robots all wrong
item.description: Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe critiques the prevailing approach to robotics development. He argues for a fundamental shift in how the industry builds and deploys autonomous systems.

item.title: Why Travis Kalanick believes humans are on the verge of a 'golden age'
item.description: Travis Kalanick argues that a convergence of technologies is setting the stage for a 'golden age' for humanity. His perspective, reported by Business Insider, frames this as a pivotal moment for innovation and commercial application.

item.title: Data security is the foundation of trust in physical AI
item.description: Emphasizes data security as the critical foundation for trust in physical AI systems. This focus addresses core operational and commercial vulnerabilities as robots move into sensitive environments.

item.title: 'I never left': Travis Kalanick launches new robotics company Atoms with manifesto
item.description: Travis Kalanick has launched a new robotics company called Atoms, accompanied by a public manifesto. The move marks his return to the tech sector with a focus on robotics commercialization.

item.title: Uber co-founder Kalanick launches Atoms in specialized robotics push
item.description: Uber co-founder Travis Kalanick has launched Atoms, a new venture focused on specialized robotics. The move signals a significant strategic push into a targeted segment of the automation market.

item.title: Three companies demonstrate global commercialization potential at AW 2026
item.description: Three robotics companies demonstrated significant progress toward global commercialization at AW 2026. Their showcases highlight a tangible shift from pilot projects to scalable, real-world deployment.

item.title: STMicroelectronics plans robots, retraining to avoid closures
item.description: STMicroelectronics is deploying robotics and retraining programs to keep its European chip plants open. The move counters high energy costs and aims to preserve its manufacturing footprint.

item.title: Elon Musk says Tesla expects to increase its human workforce as AI and robotics boost productivity
item.description: Tesla plans to expand its human workforce, citing productivity gains from AI and robotics. This hiring strategy contrasts with typical automation narratives.

item.title: Rhoda AI exits stealth with $450M to train robots from video
item.description: Rhoda AI has emerged from stealth with a $450 million funding round. The capital is earmarked for training robots using video data.

item.title: MassRobotics, NVIDIA, and AWS announce second Physical AI Fellowship cohort
item.description: MassRobotics, NVIDIA, and AWS have selected the second cohort for their Physical AI Fellowship. The program supports early-stage startups working on embodied AI and robotics.

item.title: Humanoid robotics maker Sunday reaches $1.15B valuation to build household robots
item.description: Humanoid robotics startup Sunday has reached a $1.15 billion valuation. The capital will fund its push to develop robots for household applications.

item.title: Rivian spin-out Mind Robotics raises $500M for industrial AI-powered robots
item.description: Mind Robotics, a spin-out from Rivian, has secured $500 million in funding. The capital is designated for developing industrial robots powered by AI.

item.title: Agility, Boston Dynamics, ASTM to discuss the state of humanoid robotics
item.description: Agility Robotics, Boston Dynamics, and standards body ASTM International will hold a panel on the state of humanoid robotics. The discussion will focus on the current capabilities and deployment challenges for these advanced systems.

item.title: Teradyne sues Chinese cobot maker over UR software
item.description: Teradyne has filed a lawsuit against a Chinese collaborative robot manufacturer, alleging infringement of Universal Robots' software. The legal action centers on the core programming that defines the UR cobot platform's functionality.

item.title: OpenAI robotics head resigns over Pentagon deal
item.description: A senior robotics departure at OpenAI matters because leadership changes around defense-linked decisions can reshape how teams, partners, and observers read the company's priorities in physical AI.

item.title: Plug-and-Play AI: Transforming robotics with modular skills
item.description: Modular robotics skills matter because physical AI becomes more deployable when teams can reuse capabilities across tasks instead of rebuilding each workflow from scratch.

item.title: These robots are coming for the jobs no one wants — and could fill workforce gaps
item.description: Workforce-gap robotics stories matter because automation wins fastest where jobs are repetitive, difficult to staff, or operationally unattractive rather than where robots chase the broadest labor replacement narrative.

item.title: Humanoid developer Agility Robotics rebrands
item.description: An Agility Robotics rebrand matters because company positioning in the humanoid market now affects recruiting, customer expectations, and how seriously operators interpret the path from pilot programs to scaled deployments.

item.title: Humanoid warehouse rollouts are shifting from pilot to operations in 2026
item.description: Warehouse humanoids are entering a stricter phase in 2026: operators are moving beyond pilot headlines and starting to judge programs on throughput, intervention rate, safety, and real workflow fit.

item.title: 6 lessons I learned watching a robotics startup die from the inside
item.description: Robotics startup failure stories matter because execution risk in this industry usually comes from focus, deployment discipline, and operating burn, not from a lack of technical ambition alone.

item.title: Why China’s humanoid robot industry is winning the early market
item.description: China's early lead in humanoid robotics is starting to look structural: dense supply chains, faster hardware iteration, and public pilot visibility are helping local teams move faster than many rivals.

item.title: How to integrate collaborative robots into existing production lines without disruption
item.description: Collaborative robot integration works best when teams redesign flow, safety, and operator handoffs around a real bottleneck instead of dropping a cobot into the line as a stand-alone demo.

item.title: Vision-language-action models are the next leap in autonomous robotics
item.description: Vision-language-action models matter because they promise to connect perception, language, and control in one robotics stack, but the real test is whether they reduce task engineering outside demo conditions.

item.title: AI’s role in the future of robotics: Insights from 3Laws
item.description: AI only becomes valuable in robotics when it reduces brittle task engineering, improves deployment speed, and gives operators more reliable behavior outside controlled demos.

item.title: Intrinsic is joining Google to advance physical AI in robotics
item.description: Intrinsic joining Google matters because physical AI may be moving from a specialized robotics-software effort into a broader platform bet around models, tooling, and developer leverage.

item.title: VDMA warns Germany is losing ground in global robotics race
item.description: VDMA's warning matters because Germany's robotics position now depends on whether engineering strength can still convert into faster investment, commercialization, and industrial demand as global competitors accelerate.

item.title: AI2 Robotics raises Series B funding to advance AlphaBot, embodied AI
item.description: AI2 Robotics funding matters because embodied AI teams still need capital to turn research-heavy physical AI claims into shippable products and repeatable customer value.

item.title: The hidden infrastructure challenge facing outdoor robotics OEMs
item.description: Outdoor robotics infrastructure matters because physical AI does not win on models alone; deployments also depend on charging, communications, mapping, and service assumptions that many teams underestimate.

item.title: Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada to deploy Agility Robotics’ Digit humanoids
item.description: Toyota's planned Digit deployment matters because warehouse humanoids only become durable market signals when serious industrial operators are willing to test them inside production environments.

item.title: NORD releases digital twin simulation platform for robotics developers
item.description: Digital twin tooling matters because robot learning and physical AI only scale when developers can test layouts, controls, and edge cases before touching production hardware.

item.title: Ottonomy launches Ottumn.AI to orchestrate robots, drones, and smart infrastructure
item.description: Ottumn.AI matters because orchestration layers may become part of the physical AI stack as mixed fleets of robots, drones, and infrastructure nodes need one control surface.

item.title: igus launches a new RBTX shopping platform for robotics developers
item.description: Developer marketplaces matter because faster robotics builds require easier sourcing and integration of the components that sit underneath physical AI demos and real deployments.

item.title: Amazon's Blue Jay halt shows warehouse robotics programs are being held to tighter ROI standards
item.description: Amazon's Blue Jay halt is a useful reality check for the warehouse robotics market: large operators are tightening ROI expectations and dropping programs that cannot prove practical value quickly enough.

item.title: Unitree momentum signals China is becoming a serious global robotics competitor
item.description: Unitree is becoming one of the clearest signals that China's robotics sector can compete globally on speed, visibility, and cost-aware product iteration.

item.title: China's humanoid robots take centre stage for Lunar New Year showtime
item.description: Humanoid appearances during Lunar New Year programming were more than a novelty moment. They showed how China's robot makers are using mass-audience visibility to normalize the category and build confidence around domestic progress.

item.title: Humanoid Deployments Enter the Factory Proof Stage in 2026
item.description: Humanoid deployments are entering the factory-proof stage, where performance has to survive real operating constraints instead of presentation-stage conditions.
